questions.stem:
—Have you ever ______ to the Great Wall?
—Yes. I ______ there last summer with my family.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空：'have been to'表示'去过某地（已回来）'，'have gone to'表示'去了某地（未回来）'。根据语境，对话双方都在场，说明已返回，用'been'。第二空：'last summer'是明确的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时'went'，不能用现在完成时。解题口诀：'过去时间用过去，经历去过用完成'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ______ is wearing a blue coat and riding a bike is my cousin from Hangzhou.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词'the boy'指人，且在从句中作主语（is wearing...），因此用'who'。'which'用于指物；'whom'用于指人但作宾语；'whose'表示所属关系（...的）。判断技巧：先看先行词是人还是物，再看关系词在从句中充当什么成分（主语、宾语或定语）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The new school library near our classroom ______ next month. It will be very modern.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语'the new school library'与动词'build'是被动关系（图书馆被建），因此必须用被动语态'be + 过去分词'。'next month'表示将来时间，所以用'will be built'。A和D是主动语态，C是现在进行时的主动语态，均不符合。解题技巧：先判断主语与动词是主动还是被动，再看时间确定时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: I wonder ______ Tom ______ to the party tomorrow. If he comes, I will be very happy.
questions.explanation: 本题考查宾语从句的引导词和时态。第一空：'wonder'表示'想知道'，后接宾语从句表示'是否'时用'if'或'whether'，不能用'that'（that无实义，仅起连接作用）。第二空：宾语从句中'tomorrow'是将来时间状语，需用一般将来时'will come'。注意区分：if引导宾语从句时意为'是否'，引导条件状语从句时意为'如果'（主将从现）。此处是宾语从句，作wonder的宾语。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: It's important for us to ______ the balance between study and rest during the busy exam season.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词词义辨析及固定搭配。'keep the balance'是固定搭配，意为'保持平衡'。'hold'强调'抓住、握住'，不与balance搭配；'make the balance'不是常用表达；'let'表示'让'，语义不符。类似搭配还有'keep healthy'（保持健康）、'keep quiet'（保持安静）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: If you add the prefix 'un-' to the word 'healthy', you get a new word with the ______ meaning.
questions.explanation: 本题考查前缀的构词法。前缀'un-'加在形容词前通常表示否定意义，构成反义词，如'healthy'（健康的）→'unhealthy'（不健康的），两者意思相反。其他常见否定前缀还有'dis-'（如dislike）、'im-'（如impossible）。因此'un-'改变词根的意义为相反含义。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As good classmates, we should ______ each other and help those who are in trouble.
questions.explanation: 本题考查动词短语辨析。'care about'意为'关心、在意'，符合语境'关心彼此'。'look forward to'意为'期待'（后接名词或动名词）；'look up to'意为'尊敬、敬仰'；'cheer up'意为'使振作起来'。根据句意'帮助有困难的人'，前句应该是'互相关心'，因此选C。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 本题考查时间介词的用法。在具体的某一天（如a sunny Friday morning）前要用介词'on'。'in'用于年、月、季节或泛指的上午/下午/晚上；'at'用于具体时刻；'for'表示持续时间。解题口诀：'on天on日on星期，具体某天也用on'。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) is becoming more and more important in our daily life. Last week, our school organized an activity to teach us how to sort waste properly.

The activity was held (8)______ a sunny Friday morning. A speaker from the environmental protection station came to our school. He told us that different kinds of waste should be put into different bins. For example, leftover food belongs to kitchen waste, while old newspapers and plastic bottles are recyclable waste.

Then, we (9)______ into small groups to play a game. Each group got some cards with waste names written on them. We needed to put these cards into the right 'bins' on the floor. At first, we made some mistakes. My classmate Tom put a broken glass bottle into the 'harmful waste' bin, (10)______ actually it should go into the 'recyclable' bin. Our teacher explained that glass can be recycled and (11)______ to make new bottles.

Through this activity, we realized that waste sorting is (12)______ than we thought. It is a simple action, but it can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。主语'we'与动词'divide'（分组）是被动关系，我们'被分成'小组，因此用被动语态'be divided'。文章整体叙述上周的活动，用过去时，所以是'were divided'。A是主动语态，C是动词原形，D是现在分词，均不能独立作谓语。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) is becoming more and more important in our daily life. Last week, our school organized an activity to teach us how to sort waste properly.

The activity was held (8)______ a sunny Friday morning. A speaker from the environmental protection station came to our school. He told us that different kinds of waste should be put into different bins. For example, leftover food belongs to kitchen waste, while old newspapers and plastic bottles are recyclable waste.

Then, we (9)______ into small groups to play a game. Each group got some cards with waste names written on them. We needed to put these cards into the right 'bins' on the floor. At first, we made some mistakes. My classmate Tom put a broken glass bottle into the 'harmful waste' bin, (10)______ actually it should go into the 'recyclable' bin. Our teacher explained that glass can be recycled and (11)______ to make new bottles.

Through this activity, we realized that waste sorting is (12)______ than we thought. It is a simple action, but it can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查连词的逻辑关系。前句说'Tom把破玻璃瓶放入有害垃圾箱'，后句说'实际上它应该放入可回收箱'，前后是转折关系，用'but'（但是）。'and'表并列，'or'表选择/否则，'so'表结果，均不符合语境。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) is becoming more and more important in our daily life. Last week, our school organized an activity to teach us how to sort waste properly.

The activity was held (8)______ a sunny Friday morning. A speaker from the environmental protection station came to our school. He told us that different kinds of waste should be put into different bins. For example, leftover food belongs to kitchen waste, while old newspapers and plastic bottles are recyclable waste.

Then, we (9)______ into small groups to play a game. Each group got some cards with waste names written on them. We needed to put these cards into the right 'bins' on the floor. At first, we made some mistakes. My classmate Tom put a broken glass bottle into the 'harmful waste' bin, (10)______ actually it should go into the 'recyclable' bin. Our teacher explained that glass can be recycled and (11)______ to make new bottles.

Through this activity, we realized that waste sorting is (12)______ than we thought. It is a simple action, but it can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查被动语态。主语'glass'与'use'是被动关系，且前面有'be recycled and'，and连接并列结构，因此后面也用过去分词'used'，构成'can be recycled and used'（能够被回收和用来...）。B是原形，C是现在分词，D是三单形式，均不符合被动语态结构。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) is becoming more and more important in our daily life. Last week, our school organized an activity to teach us how to sort waste properly.

The activity was held (8)______ a sunny Friday morning. A speaker from the environmental protection station came to our school. Through this activity, we realized that waste sorting is (12)______ than we thought. It is a simple action, but it can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.explanation: 本题考查形容词比较级。句中有'than'（比），这是比较级的标志词，因此要用比较级'easier'。A是原级，C和D是最高级，均不能与'than'连用。多音节形容词前加more构成比较级，但easy是双音节词，变y为i加-er。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) is becoming more and more important in our daily life. Last week, our school organized an activity to teach us how to sort waste properly.

The activity was held (8)______ a sunny Friday morning. A speaker from the environmental protection station came to our school. He told us that different kinds of waste should be put into different bins. For example, leftover food belongs to kitchen waste, while old newspapers and plastic bottles are recyclable waste.

Our teacher explained that glass can be recycled and (11)______ to make new bottles.

Through this activity, we realized that waste sorting is (12)______ than we thought. It is a simple action, but it can make a big difference to our environment.

questions.stem: When was the first Vertical Forest built according to the passage?
questions.explanation: 本题是细节理解题。根据文章第二段第二句'The first Vertical Forest was built in Milan, Italy in 2014.'可知，第一座垂直森林建于2014年。细节题解题技巧：先读题干找关键词（如first Vertical Forest, built），然后回原文定位相关句子，比对选项即可。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
In big cities, there are more and more tall buildings but fewer and fewer green plants. To solve this problem, Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed a special kind of building called 'Vertical Forest' (垂直森林).

These buildings look like giant trees. They are covered with thousands of plants, including trees, shrubs and flowers. The first Vertical Forest was built in Milan, Italy in 2014. It has two towers, 80 and 112 meters tall. In total, there are about 900 trees and 20,000 plants on them.

Many people wonder how these plants can survive on tall buildings. Actually, each plant has its own special soil and watering system. The building's design also considers wind and sunlight. Strong winds at high places might blow down the trees, so the architects choose special tree species with deep roots.

Vertical Forests bring many benefits to cities. First, they absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, improving air quality. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects, increasing biodiversity. Third, they help reduce noise pollution and keep the building cool in summer.

However, some people worry about the high cost of building and maintaining such buildings. Others are afraid that falling leaves and branches might be dangerous. Despite these concerns, more Vertical Forests are being built around the world, including in China.

questions.stem: What does the underlined word 'absorbing' in Paragraph 4 most probably mean?
questions.explanation: 本题是词义猜测题。根据上下文语境，第四段讲垂直森林的好处，提到'absorbing CO2 and produce oxygen'（吸收二氧化碳并产生氧气）。'absorb'与'produce'（产生）相对，应该是'吸收'的意思。'take in'表示'吸收、摄入'，与'give out'（释放）相反。解题技巧：通过并列结构'absorbing... and produce...'以及常识（植物吸收CO2释放O2）来推断词义。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
In big cities, there are more and more tall buildings but fewer and fewer green plants. To solve this problem, Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed a special kind of building called 'Vertical Forest' (垂直森林).

These buildings look like giant trees. They are covered with thousands of plants, including trees, shrubs and flowers. The first Vertical Forest was built in Milan, Italy in 2014. It has two towers, 80 and 112 meters tall. In total, there are about 900 trees and 20,000 plants on them.

Many people wonder how these plants can survive on tall buildings. Actually, each plant has its own special soil and watering system. The building's design also considers wind and sunlight. Strong winds at high places might blow down the trees, so the architects choose special tree species with deep roots.

Vertical Forests bring many benefits to cities. First, they absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, improving air quality. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects, increasing biodiversity. Third, they help reduce noise pollution and keep the building cool in summer.

However, some people worry about the high cost of building and maintaining such buildings. Others are afraid that falling leaves and branches might be dangerous. Despite these concerns, more Vertical Forests are being built around the world, including in China.

questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage about Vertical Forests?
options: A. They are cheap to build and maintain
B. They have both advantages and disadvantages
C. Any kind of tree can grow well on these buildings
D. They do not need special watering systems
questions.explanation: 本题是推理判断题。文章第四段讲垂直森林的好处（吸收CO2、提供生物栖息地、减少噪音等），第五段讲人们的担忧（成本高、维护难、落叶危险等），说明垂直森林既有优点也有缺点。A与原文'high cost'矛盾；C与原文'choose special tree species'矛盾；D与原文'watering system'矛盾。推理题要注意整合全文信息，不能只看某一段。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
In big cities, there are more and more tall buildings but fewer and fewer green plants. To solve this problem, Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed a special kind of building called 'Vertical Forest' (垂直森林).

These buildings look like giant trees. They are covered with thousands of plants, including trees, shrubs and flowers. The first Vertical Forest was built in Milan, Italy in 2014. It has two towers, 80 and 112 meters tall. In total, there are about 900 trees and 20,000 plants on them.

Many people wonder how these plants can survive on tall buildings. Actually, each plant has its own special soil and watering system. The building's design also considers wind and sunlight. Strong winds at high places might blow down the trees, so the architects choose special tree species with deep roots.

Vertical Forests bring many benefits to cities. First, they absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, improving air quality. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects, increasing biodiversity. Third, they help reduce noise pollution and keep the building cool in summer.

However, some people worry about the high cost of building and maintaining such buildings. Others are afraid that falling leaves and branches might be dangerous. Despite these concerns, more Vertical Forests are being built around the world, including in China.

questions.stem: What is the writer's attitude towards Vertical Forests?
questions.explanation: 本题考查作者态度。虽然文章提到了垂直森林的一些缺点（第五段），但最后一句'Despite these concerns, more Vertical Forests are being built around the world'（尽管有担忧，但越来越多垂直森林在建造）表明作者对这种建筑持支持态度。此外，文章用大量篇幅介绍其环保益处，整体基调是积极的。解题技巧：关注文章首尾段和转折词后的内容，'despite'（尽管）强调后半句的内容更重要。
questions.label: 阅读-作者态度
questions.group_passage:
In big cities, there are more and more tall buildings but fewer and fewer green plants. To solve this problem, Italian architect Stefano Boeri designed a special kind of building called 'Vertical Forest' (垂直森林).

These buildings look like giant trees. They are covered with thousands of plants, including trees, shrubs and flowers. The first Vertical Forest was built in Milan, Italy in 2014. It has two towers, 80 and 112 meters tall. In total, there are about 900 trees and 20,000 plants on them.

Many people wonder how these plants can survive on tall buildings. Actually, each plant has its own special soil and watering system. The building's design also considers wind and sunlight. Strong winds at high places might blow down the trees, so the architects choose special tree species with deep roots.

Vertical Forests bring many benefits to cities. First, they absorb CO2 and produce oxygen, improving air quality. Second, they provide homes for birds and insects, increasing biodiversity. Third, they help reduce noise pollution and keep the building cool in summer.

However, some people worry about the high cost of building and maintaining such buildings. Others are afraid that falling leaves and branches might be dangerous. Despite these concerns, more Vertical Forests are being built around the world, including in China.

options: A. First, choose a topic that you are interested in.
B. Second, organize your ideas clearly.
C. However, don't write every word on the paper.
E. In addition, try to speak slowly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接与逻辑顺序。空后提到'First, choose...'，说明此处应该是演讲准备的第一步。选项A'First, choose a topic...'与后文的'First'呼应，且'choose a topic'（选话题）是准备演讲的第一步，符合逻辑顺序。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
How to Prepare for an English Speech

Giving an English speech can be challenging, but with good preparation, you can do it well. Here are some useful tips.

(16)______ First, choose a topic that you are interested in. If you love the topic, you will be more confident when talking about it.

(17)______ Second, organize your ideas clearly. Write down the main points and supporting details. Make sure your speech has a beginning, a middle, and an end.

(18)______ However, don't write every word on the paper. Just use key words to remind yourself. If you read the whole speech, the audience will get bored.

(19)______ Finally, practice makes perfect. Rehearse your speech several times before the big day. You can practice in front of a mirror or record yourself.

(20)______ Remember, everyone feels nervous before speaking. Just take a deep breath and believe in yourself. Good luck!

options: A. First, choose a topic that you are interested in.
B. Second, organize your ideas clearly.
C. However, don't write every word on the paper.
E. In addition, try to speak slowly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 本题考查段落衔接。空后出现'Second, organize...'，说明此处应该是第二步的总起句。选项B'Second, organize your ideas clearly'与后文的'Second'完全对应，且'organize ideas'（组织思路）是选好话题后的第二步，逻辑连贯。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:

(17)______ Second, organize your ideas clearly. Write down the main points and supporting details. Make sure your speech has a beginning, a middle, and an end.

(18)______ However, don't write every word on the paper. Just use key words to remind yourself. If you read the whole speech, the audience will get bored.

(19)______ Finally, practice makes perfect. Rehearse your speech several times before the big day. You can practice in front of a mirror or record yourself.

(20)______ Remember, everyone feels nervous before speaking. Just take a deep breath and believe in yourself. Good luck!

options: A. First, choose a topic that you are interested in.
B. Second, organize your ideas clearly.
C. However, don't write every word on the paper.
E. In addition, try to speak slowly and clearly.
questions.explanation: 本题考查转折关系。空前讲'写下要点和细节'，空后讲'However, don't write every word...'（不要把每个字都写下来），说明此处与后文内容一致，共同构成转折关系。选项C'However, don't write every word on the paper'正好与后文完全对应，其中'However'表示转折，提醒不要写全文。
